nodes.name: fm.client_side_rbac_gate
nodes.summary:
An AI agent adds a permission check in the UI as the primary guard for a
sensitive action. The server-side interceptor is still the real enforcer,
but the UI check creates a false sense of security and may be bypassed by
direct API calls or by manipulating local state.

nodes.name: ui.destructive_action_requires_explicit_confirmation
nodes.summary:
Actions that are hard or impossible to reverse — node removal, service deletion,
RBAC role deletion, artifact deletion, objectstore topology reset — must present
an explicit confirmation step before dispatching the mutating RPC.
The confirmation must name the resource being mutated and the consequence.
A single-click "Delete" button with no confirmation dialog is a violation.

nodes.name: metric_warning:etcd_fsync_latency_ms
nodes.summary: High etcd fsync latency risks leader election instability and workflow dispatch timeouts

metadata.explanation: High etcd fsync latency risks leader election instability and workflow dispatch timeouts

nodes.name: fm.silent_grpc_error
nodes.summary:
A component catches a gRPC-web error but does not surface it to the operator.
The UI stays in a loading state, shows an empty list, or renders the last
cached value. The operator has no signal that the backend is unreachable or
that the RPC failed.

nodes.name: fm.dispatch_shown_as_complete
nodes.summary:
After the UI dispatches a mutating RPC (join approval, service deploy, topology
apply), it immediately renders Success without waiting for the workflow to reach
a terminal state. The operation may fail 30 seconds later while the operator
believes it succeeded.

nodes.name: ui.super_admin_detection_is_display_only
nodes.summary:
The SDK detects super-admin by checking if the parsed token's username equals 'sa'.
This is used to show or hide admin-only UI sections — it is NOT a security check.
Server-side RBAC enforces what 'sa' can actually do. An AI agent must not elevate
this client-side check into an authorization gate or rely on it for security decisions.

nodes.name: ui.token_storage_sessionStorage_only
nodes.summary:
The JWT auth token must be stored in sessionStorage (cleared on tab close),
never in localStorage (persists across sessions) or in DOM attributes / JS globals.
Persisting tokens in localStorage makes them available to XSS and across
unrelated sessions — violating the ephemeral token contract.
Current implementation: sessionStorage key __globular_token__.

nodes.summary: etcd disk full triggers NOSPACE alarm, blocks all writes, halts convergence

nodes.name: ui.workflow_completion_requires_terminal_state_poll
nodes.summary:
When the UI dispatches a cluster mutation (service deploy, node join approval,
topology apply, package install), the dispatch RPC returning 200 does NOT mean the
operation completed. The workflow may still be running, blocked, or about to fail.
The UI must poll WorkflowService for SUCCEEDED or FAILED before showing a success
state. Showing "Done ✓" immediately after dispatch is a violation.

nodes.summary: Workflow failures indicate convergence risk and may threaten reconcile fairness

nodes.name: fm.desired_shown_as_running
nodes.summary:
A UI component reads the DesiredService record from etcd and displays it as
running or healthy. The service may actually be absent, crashed, or in the
middle of a converge cycle. Operators see a false green and miss real outages.

nodes.name: ui.grpc_web_errors_must_surface_to_operator
nodes.summary:
The SDK auto-retries on auth expiry (one forced refresh + retry). After that,
any RPC error must be presented to the operator — not silently dropped, not
shown as "loading" indefinitely, not defaulted to empty/healthy state.
Swallowing errors hides real cluster problems from operators.

nodes.name: fm.no_confirmation_on_destructive_action
nodes.summary:
A delete, wipe, or remove action is reachable with a single click and no
confirmation dialog. The operator can accidentally remove a node, delete a role,
or wipe an artifact without a chance to cancel.

nodes.name: ui.no_hardcoded_backend_addresses
nodes.summary:
The Globular backend address is stored in localStorage (globular.baseUrl) and
routing mode in localStorage (globular.routingMode). These values allow the
admin UI to target different clusters without a rebuild.
An AI agent must not hardcode a backend address (IP, hostname, or port) in the
source code. If a component needs to call a backend, it must read the address
from the SDK config layer.

nodes.summary: Offline MinIO disks risk artifact availability and violate topology contract

nodes.name: ui.unknown_state_must_not_appear_healthy
nodes.summary:
When a health probe times out, an inventory record is absent, or a node is
unreachable, the UI must show "unknown" or "unreachable" — never "healthy" or "ok".
Defaulting unknown to healthy hides real outages from operators.

nodes.name: ui.rbac_is_server_enforced_not_ui
nodes.summary:
The admin UI hides action buttons for users who lack permission. This is a UX
convenience only — it is not a security boundary. The server enforces RBAC on
every RPC via the interceptor chain.
An AI agent must not add client-side permission checks as the sole security gate
for a sensitive action. If a button is hidden, the underlying RPC endpoint must
still enforce authorization independently.

nodes.name: 4-Layer State Model
nodes.summary: 4-Layer State Model

nodes.name: Protocol Buffers
nodes.summary: Protocol Buffers

nodes.name: ui.state_authority_binding
nodes.summary:
Every badge, status indicator, action button, and progress element in the
admin UI must derive its displayed value from the correct authority layer.
The 4-layer model applies: desired ≠ installed ≠ running ≠ healthy.
A component that renders service status must bind to a runtime health probe,
not to the desired-state record. A workflow-progress indicator must bind to
the workflow terminal state, not to the dispatch RPC return code.

nodes.name: ui.join_approval_starts_workflow_not_completes_join
nodes.summary:
Calling approveJoin(requestId, profiles) dispatches a join workflow.
The node is NOT joined when the RPC returns. The operator UI must show the
workflow progress (pending → converging → joined) and must not display the
node as a cluster member until the join workflow reaches SUCCEEDED and the
node's first heartbeat is observed.

nodes.name: fm.hardcoded_backend_address
nodes.summary:
A component or SDK module constructs a gRPC-web channel with a hardcoded
address. The admin UI becomes tied to a specific cluster, breaks in
dev/staging/prod, and prevents targeting different nodes.

nodes.name: fm.token_leaked_to_localStorage
nodes.summary:
A change to the auth or token-refresh logic writes the JWT to localStorage.
The token persists across browser sessions, is readable by any JS on the origin,
and survives logout (which only clears sessionStorage).

invariants.title: Every visible state must be bound to the correct authority layer
invariants.summary:
Every badge, status indicator, action button, and progress element in the
admin UI must derive its displayed value from the correct authority layer.
The 4-layer model applies: desired ≠ installed ≠ running ≠ healthy.
A component that renders service status must bind to a runtime health probe,
not to the desired-state record. A workflow-progress indicator must bind to
the workflow terminal state, not to the dispatch RPC return code.

invariants.title: Unknown or unreachable state must render as unknown, not healthy
invariants.summary:
When a health probe times out, an inventory record is absent, or a node is
unreachable, the UI must show "unknown" or "unreachable" — never "healthy" or "ok".
Defaulting unknown to healthy hides real outages from operators.

invariants.title: Super-admin detection via username === 'sa' is a display hint, not an auth gate
invariants.summary:
The SDK detects super-admin by checking if the parsed token's username equals 'sa'.
This is used to show or hide admin-only UI sections — it is NOT a security check.
Server-side RBAC enforces what 'sa' can actually do. An AI agent must not elevate
this client-side check into an authorization gate or rely on it for security decisions.

invariants.title: Approving a node join request starts a workflow — the node is not joined until convergence
invariants.summary:
Calling approveJoin(requestId, profiles) dispatches a join workflow.
The node is NOT joined when the RPC returns. The operator UI must show the
workflow progress (pending → converging → joined) and must not display the
node as a cluster member until the join workflow reaches SUCCEEDED and the
node's first heartbeat is observed.

invariants.title: Destructive or irreversible actions must require explicit operator confirmation
invariants.summary:
Actions that are hard or impossible to reverse — node removal, service deletion,
RBAC role deletion, artifact deletion, objectstore topology reset — must present
an explicit confirmation step before dispatching the mutating RPC.
The confirmation must name the resource being mutated and the consequence.
A single-click "Delete" button with no confirmation dialog is a violation.

invariants.title: Auth tokens must be stored in sessionStorage, never localStorage or DOM
invariants.summary:
The JWT auth token must be stored in sessionStorage (cleared on tab close),
never in localStorage (persists across sessions) or in DOM attributes / JS globals.
Persisting tokens in localStorage makes them available to XSS and across
unrelated sessions — violating the ephemeral token contract.
Current implementation: sessionStorage key __globular_token__.

invariants.title: RBAC enforcement is server-side; UI may hide but must not gate security on visibility
invariants.summary:
The admin UI hides action buttons for users who lack permission. This is a UX
convenience only — it is not a security boundary. The server enforces RBAC on
every RPC via the interceptor chain.
An AI agent must not add client-side permission checks as the sole security gate
for a sensitive action. If a button is hidden, the underlying RPC endpoint must
still enforce authorization independently.

invariants.title: Workflow success must be confirmed by polling for terminal state, not from dispatch
invariants.summary:
When the UI dispatches a cluster mutation (service deploy, node join approval,
topology apply, package install), the dispatch RPC returning 200 does NOT mean the
operation completed. The workflow may still be running, blocked, or about to fail.
The UI must poll WorkflowService for SUCCEEDED or FAILED before showing a success
state. Showing "Done ✓" immediately after dispatch is a violation.

invariants.title: gRPC-web errors must be surfaced to the operator, not silently swallowed
invariants.summary:
The SDK auto-retries on auth expiry (one forced refresh + retry). After that,
any RPC error must be presented to the operator — not silently dropped, not
shown as "loading" indefinitely, not defaulted to empty/healthy state.
Swallowing errors hides real cluster problems from operators.

invariants.title: Backend addresses must come from localStorage config, not hardcoded
invariants.summary:
The Globular backend address is stored in localStorage (globular.baseUrl) and
routing mode in localStorage (globular.routingMode). These values allow the
admin UI to target different clusters without a rebuild.
An AI agent must not hardcode a backend address (IP, hostname, or port) in the
source code. If a component needs to call a backend, it must read the address
from the SDK config layer.

failure_modes.title: gRPC-web error silently dropped — UI shows stale or empty state
failure_modes.summary:
A component catches a gRPC-web error but does not surface it to the operator.
The UI stays in a loading state, shows an empty list, or renders the last
cached value. The operator has no signal that the backend is unreachable or
that the RPC failed.
symptoms: Component shows empty table with no error message
Spinner never resolves
Status badge stays in loading state indefinitely
Console has gRPC errors; UI shows no indicator
failure_modes.root_cause:
A component catches a gRPC-web error but does not surface it to the operator.
The UI stays in a loading state, shows an empty list, or renders the last
cached value. The operator has no signal that the backend is unreachable or
that the RPC failed.

failure_modes.title: Client-side RBAC check used as the sole security gate
failure_modes.summary:
An AI agent adds a permission check in the UI as the primary guard for a
sensitive action. The server-side interceptor is still the real enforcer,
but the UI check creates a false sense of security and may be bypassed by
direct API calls or by manipulating local state.
symptoms: Button is hidden for non-admin users but the underlying RPC has no authz annotation
Security audit finds UI-only guards for data deletion endpoints
failure_modes.root_cause:
An AI agent adds a permission check in the UI as the primary guard for a
sensitive action. The server-side interceptor is still the real enforcer,
but the UI check creates a false sense of security and may be bypassed by
direct API calls or by manipulating local state.

failure_modes.title: Destructive action dispatched without operator confirmation
failure_modes.summary:
A delete, wipe, or remove action is reachable with a single click and no
confirmation dialog. The operator can accidentally remove a node, delete a role,
or wipe an artifact without a chance to cancel.
symptoms: Delete button directly calls RPC on click without dialog
Are-you-sure dialog has no resource name or consequence description
Keyboard shortcut triggers destructive action with no confirmation
failure_modes.root_cause:
A delete, wipe, or remove action is reachable with a single click and no
confirmation dialog. The operator can accidentally remove a node, delete a role,
or wipe an artifact without a chance to cancel.

failure_modes.title: Backend address hardcoded in source instead of resolved from config
failure_modes.summary:
A component or SDK module constructs a gRPC-web channel with a hardcoded
address. The admin UI becomes tied to a specific cluster, breaks in
dev/staging/prod, and prevents targeting different nodes.
symptoms: UI targets wrong cluster in non-production environment
Changing the cluster address requires a code rebuild
SDK config layer is bypassed for specific endpoints
failure_modes.root_cause:
A component or SDK module constructs a gRPC-web channel with a hardcoded
address. The admin UI becomes tied to a specific cluster, breaks in
dev/staging/prod, and prevents targeting different nodes.

failure_modes.title: Desired state displayed as runtime health
failure_modes.summary:
A UI component reads the DesiredService record from etcd and displays it as
running or healthy. The service may actually be absent, crashed, or in the
middle of a converge cycle. Operators see a false green and miss real outages.
symptoms: Service status badge shows running for a service that is not active on any node
Node convergence failures are invisible to the operator
All-services-healthy dashboard while cluster is partially down
failure_modes.root_cause:
A UI component reads the DesiredService record from etcd and displays it as
running or healthy. The service may actually be absent, crashed, or in the
middle of a converge cycle. Operators see a false green and miss real outages.

failure_modes.title: Workflow dispatch shown as operation complete
failure_modes.summary:
After the UI dispatches a mutating RPC (join approval, service deploy, topology
apply), it immediately renders Success without waiting for the workflow to reach
a terminal state. The operation may fail 30 seconds later while the operator
believes it succeeded.
symptoms: Deploy-successful toast appears but service never starts
Node-joined UI but node is still converging or failed
Operator navigates away assuming completion and misses failure
failure_modes.root_cause:
After the UI dispatches a mutating RPC (join approval, service deploy, topology
apply), it immediately renders Success without waiting for the workflow to reach
a terminal state. The operation may fail 30 seconds later while the operator
believes it succeeded.

failure_modes.title: Auth token written to localStorage instead of sessionStorage
failure_modes.summary:
A change to the auth or token-refresh logic writes the JWT to localStorage.
The token persists across browser sessions, is readable by any JS on the origin,
and survives logout (which only clears sessionStorage).
symptoms: Token survives tab close / browser restart
Token visible under Application Local Storage in DevTools after logout
Auto-login without credential prompt after full session close
failure_modes.root_cause:
A change to the auth or token-refresh logic writes the JWT to localStorage.
The token persists across browser sessions, is readable by any JS on the origin,
and survives logout (which only clears sessionStorage).
